venues.name: Cha-no-wa
venues.description: Japanese brand's first Australian outpost. Premium matcha sourced from Kyoto during the global matcha shortage — Adelaide's love affair with matcha reached fever pitch here.
venues.mustTry: Kyoto-sourced matcha and hojicha, matcha frappes and sundaes, Japanese cakes and ice-cream

venues.name: Homeboy 2.0
venues.description: Teen entrepreneur Tom Oswald's viral Hahndorf cafe reborn in the CBD. Mum's famous cinnamon scrolls had a moment in 2025 — and they're topnotch.
venues.mustTry: Mum's famous cinnamon scrolls, focaccia toasties, Homeboy coffee blend

venues.name: Pompom
venues.description: All-day diner from Whistle & Flute crew built on fluffy flatbread fired in a Gozney oven. Riding Forestville's redevelopment wave.
venues.mustTry: The Pompom (chicken, roast capsicum, feta, tabouli), Cypriot sausage pita, whipped ricotta with spicy salami

venues.name: Meli
venues.description: Greek beach bar energy comes to Adelaide. Golden loukoumades in four flavors, Greek-inspired sandos, and desserts that make your sweet tooth dance.
venues.mustTry: Loukoumades (traditional, pistachio, biscoff, Nutella), Melizana, Saganaki Sando, Galaktoboureko Cups, Pistachio Baklava Freddo

venues.name: Yuku dō
venues.description: If you have a Japanese-inspired brunch craving, this is Adelaide's answer. Modern Japanese breakfast meets Australian cafe culture.
venues.mustTry: Japanese breakfast sets, matcha drinks, contemporary Japanese-Australian fusion plates

venues.name: Bora
venues.description: Breakfast classics and lunch staples with a Brazilian twist. Beachside location that hosted Adelaide's inaugural Brazilian festival, Alma Brazil.
venues.mustTry: The BELT (BLT with eggs, Dijon, pecorino), acai bowls, breakfast tacos

venues.name: Jenny's Bakery
venues.description: Not exactly new, but its influence built to fever pitch in 2025. Keswick location in old Sylvia's Deli site opened November to much acclaim.

venues.name: Arlos
venues.description: Fresh face in CBD's heart, taking over Cibo corner spot. Next-level coffee, fresh bites, and cinnamon scrolls that are dreamy AF.
venues.mustTry: Cinnamon scrolls (original, blueberry, frangelico), specialty coffee, fresh sandos

venues.name: Gathers & Grounds
venues.description: Asian fusion twist on this sunny corner cafe. Specializing in Hojicha, matcha, Vietnamese coffee — made its mark in just weeks.
venues.mustTry: Chicken Katsu sando, Ube hotcakes, Vietnamese coffee, matcha drinks

venues.name: Pinco Deli
venues.description: Third Pinco location proves the sando outlasted the burger trend. Italian staples meet sandwich excellence from Jumbo Smash founders.
venues.mustTry: Sando lineup, baked alla vodka pasta with stracciatella, pappardelle with ragu bianco
